2604.07119.title: Are Non-English Papers Reviewed Fairly? Language-of-Study Bias in NLP Peer Reviews
2604.07119.paper_summary: Peer review plays a central role in the NLP publication process, but is susceptible to various biases. Here, we study language-of-study (LoS) bias: the tendency for reviewers to evaluate a paper differently based on the language(s) it studies, rather than its scientific merit. Despite being explicitly flagged in reviewing guidelines, such biases are poorly understood. Prior work treats such comments as part of broader categories of weak or unconstructive reviews without defining them as a distinct form of bias. We present the first systematic characterization of LoS bias, distinguishing negative and positive forms, and introduce the human-annotated dataset LOBSTER (Language-Of-study Bias in ScienTific pEer Review) and a method achieving 87.37 macro F1 for detection. We analyze 15,645 reviews to estimate how negative and positive biases differ with respect to the LoS, and find that non-English papers face substantially higher bias rates than English-only ones, with negative bias consistently outweighing positive bias. Finally, we identify four subcategories of negative bias, and find that demanding unjustified cross-lingual generalization is the most dominant form. We publicly release all resources to support work on fairer reviewing practices in NLP and beyond.
2604.07119.paper_summary_zh: 同儕評審在自然語言處理（NLP）出版過程中扮演著核心角色，但容易受到各種偏見的影響。<lb/>在這裡，我們研究了研究語言（LoS）偏見：評審根據論文所研究的語言而非其科學價值來評估論文的傾向。<lb/>儘管在評審指南中明確標示了這種偏見，但對其理解仍然很有限。<lb/>先前的研究將此類評論視為較廣泛的弱評或不具建設性的評審的一部分，而未將其定義為一種獨特的偏見形式。<lb/>我們首次系統性地描述了LoS偏見，區分了負面和正面形式，並介紹了人類標註的數據集LOBSTER（Language-Of-study Bias in ScienTific pEer Review）以及一種達到87.37宏F1的檢測方法。<lb/>我們分析了15,645條評審，以估計負面和正面偏見在LoS方面的差異，發現非英語論文面臨的偏見率顯著高於僅使用英語的論文，且負面偏見始終超過正面偏見。<lb/>最後，我們確定了四個負面偏見的子類別，並發現要求不合理的跨語言概括是最主要的形式。<lb/>我們公開釋放所有資源，以支持在NLP及其他領域中推動更公平的評審實踐。
2604.07119.author: Ehsan Barkhordar et.al.
2604.07119.authors: Ehsan Barkhordar, Abdulfattah Safa, Verena Blaschke, Erika Lombart, Marie-Catherine de Marneffe, Gözde Gül Şahin
